msgs.text:
Turn 52 — executable verifier audit: linear backbone verified; nonlinear D.3 needs representative data I re-read the attached problem_statement.md, mega_prompt.md, and turns_history__q2_candidate_iso.md only. The problem remains the over-G isomorphism question for the quotients ΓS,ΓH, where F=P⋊G, P is the free G-operator pro-2 group on x0,x1, and the required conclusion is a continuous isomorphism Φ:ΓS→ΓH with πH∘Φ=πS.

The useful Turn 52 refinement is:the appendix verifier splits into an executable linear core and a representative-dependent nonlinear core.

Turn 51’s next task was to write an appendix verifier constructingΔˉn, Ln, ∂d, θd0, D5,6,7, and μ4B4, then print the rank tables and the D.3 rank jump.I implemented and ran the part that is genuinely canonical from the reduced-coproduct primitive-basis procedure:Δˉn, Ln, ∂d, and θd0. That executable verifier reproduces exactly the primitive dimensions, boundary ranks, and secondary quotient ranks listed in Turn 51.

I saved the verifier here:Download turn52_linear_verifier.py

Its output is:n	dimLn 1	2 2	3 3	2 4	6 5	6 6	11 7	18 8	36 9	56 10	105and d	dimLd rank∂d dimker∂d rankθd0dimHd(2)4	6	6	6	5	1 5	6	9	3	3	3 6	11	18	4	4	7 7	18	30	6	6	12 8	36	56	16	15	21 9	56	99	13	13	43 10	105	186	24	24	81 This verifies the whole linear primitive-boundary skeleton of the appendix.

The important audit correction is that D5,6,7 and μ4B4 cannot be generated from the primitive bases alone. Turn 51 phrases the remaining artifact as “generate D5,6,7 and μ4B4 from the canonical primitive-basis algorithm,” but that is only partly true: the primitive-basis algorithm fixes the vector spaces and rank targets, while the actual nonlinear matrices require additional data:actual restricted group representatives,the fixed I9 -base lift,the truncation convention for substituting into S(Y0,Y1,q),and the coefficient-Bockstein projection convention.This is not a new obstruction. It is a reproducibility boundary. The history itself repeatedly warned that actual restricted representatives, not naked primitive initial forms, are required because pro-2 representatives carry forced Bockstein tails. That warning appeared already in the residual computations, where treating a degree-four correction as a bare associative polynomial was explicitly identified as structurally wrong.

So Turn 52 changes the appendix plan slightly:A/B/C verifier is now executable.The script verifies dimLn, rank∂d, dimker∂d, rankθd0, and dimHd(2).D.3 remains an executable-hook problem, not a primitive-basis problem.The desired certificate is still the invariant stack-rank test rank(D5,6,7)=60,rank(D5,6,7μ4B4)=61.This is exactly the Turn 51 D.3 rank-jump certificate: since H4(2)is one-dimensional, the rank jump proves that the continuation kernel toggles μ4.The next reproducibility target is now precise.Write a second verifier module that imports the same primitive bases but additionally defines the actual restricted representatives and the fixed I9 -base. Only then can it construct D5,6,7 and μ4B4 honestly.This is a useful structural tightening: it avoids pretending that the nonlinear Bockstein matrix is determined by the Hopf primitive basis alone. The linear rank tables are now independently executable; the nonlinear D.3 stack-rank test is the sole remaining executable bookkeeping layer.

ESTABLISHED: The deterministic linear appendix verifier is now executable and reproduces the primitive dimensions, boundary ranks, and secondary quotient ranks used in the proof. The script is available as turn52_linear_verifier.py.

GAP: No new structural obstruction appears. The remaining reproducibility gap is that D5,6,7 and μ4B4 require actual restricted representative expansions and the fixed I9 -base; they are not generated by the primitive-basis algorithm alone.

NEXT: Build turn52_nonlinear_d3_verifier.py: define the actual restricted representatives, encode the fixed I9 -base lift, expand S(Y0,Y1,q) through degrees 6,7,8, construct D5,6,7 and μ4B4, and print the two rank checks 60,61.[TURN52-LINEAR-VERIFIER-EXECUTABLE-NONLINEAR-D3-HOOK]
